sentences.english: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
I'm Phil.
And I'm Beth. Now, let's talk about food.
OK. What food do you love?
What food do you hate?

quiz.question: What does '무의미한, 쓸데없는' mean in English?
options: I would say that, wouldn't I?

sentences.english: If you ask around, you'll soon see there's no right or wrong answer – it's all a question of taste.
But our taste, it turns out, isn't simply a matter of opinion.
sentences.korean: 하지만 알고 보면, 미각은 단순한 의견의 문제가 아닙니다.

sentences.english: Rather, scientists have discovered that taste is influenced by our genes and DNA.
So, in this programme we'll be asking, what is taste?
Why can't we agree on it?
sentences.korean: 왜 맛에 대해 동의할 수 없을까요?

quiz.question: What does '무의미한, 쓸데없는' mean in English?

sentences.english: And is it worth listening to experts whose job is to tell us what to eat and drink?
And, as usual, we'll be learning some useful new vocabulary as well.
Great, but first I have a question for you, Beth.
A good way of finding out about British tastes is with the nation's best-loved snack, crisps.
So, what is the most popular flavour of crisps in the UK?
sentences.korean: 영국에서 가장 인기 있는 과자 맛은 뭘까요?

quiz.question: What does '꾸짖다, 비난하다' mean in English?
options: I would say that, wouldn't I?

sentences.english: Is it: a) salt and vinegar? b) cheese and onion? or c) ready salted?
What a great question!
Hmm, I'm going to say b) cheese and onion.
sentences.korean: 음, b) 치즈 앤 어니언이라고 할게요.

sentences.english: OK, we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
Now, the modern study of taste goes back over 100 years, to a German scientist called Arthur Fox who was studying a chemical called PTC.

quiz.question: What does '무의미한, 쓸데없는' mean in English?

sentences.english: Here, Danielle Reed, a geneticist at the Monell Chemical Senses Center in Philadelphia, tells BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain, what happened next:
sentences.korean: 필라델피아 모넬 화학감각센터의 유전학자 대니엘 리드가 설명합니다.

sentences.english: Fox was working with PTC and it flew up into the air.
sentences.korean: 폭스가 PTC로 작업하고 있었는데 그게 공중으로 날아갔습니다.

sentences.english: And his colleague said, 'Wow, that's really bitter.' And Fox said, 'I can't taste anything.' And then they started to go around the whole lab and test everyone, and about 30% of people in the lab couldn't taste it.
sentences.korean: 동료가 '와, 정말 쓰다'고 했고, 폭스는 '아무 맛도 안 나는데'라고 했어요.

sentences.english: When scientists started looking outside Europe, so broadening their horizons, they found some populations where almost everyone was a taster and some populations where almost nobody was, and then scientists came to realise that this was much more diverse than we ever realised…
sentences.korean: 과학자들이 유럽 밖으로 시야를 넓혔을 때, 거의 모든 사람이 맛을 느끼는 인구 집단을 발견했습니다.

sentences.english: And what you're saying is, "No, taste isn't a matter of opinion, it's a matter of biology".
sentences.korean: 당신이 말하는 건, '미각은 의견의 문제가 아니라 생물학의 문제'라는 거죠.

quiz.question: What does '내가 그렇게 말할 수밖에 (편향 인정)' mean in English?
options: I would say that, wouldn't I?

sentences.english: Exactly and we wouldn't... you know, for our friends who are colour-blind, we wouldn't chastise them for not being able to see red or for dressing in colours we don't appreciate, but we feel very free to criticise our friends' sense of taste.
It was only when scientists looked outside Europe to the rest of the world that they realised the diversity of human taste.
sentences.korean: 과학자들이 유럽 밖 세계를 살펴봤을 때 비로소 인간 미각의 다양성을 깨달았습니다.

sentences.english: Danielle says they broadened their horizons, they investigated something in a new way to increase their knowledge and understanding of it.
Danielle compares taste to another genetically controlled condition, being colour-blind or unable to see the difference between certain colours like green and red.
sentences.korean: 대니엘은 미각을 또 다른 유전적으로 결정되는 상태인 색맹과 비교합니다.

sentences.english: You wouldn't chastise – criticise or punish - someone for being colour-blind, so why criticise someone's taste?
sentences.korean: 색맹인 사람을 꾸짖거나 — 비판하거나 벌하지 — 않잖아요.

quiz.question: What does '내가 그렇게 말할 수밖에 (편향 인정)' mean in English?
options: I would say that, wouldn't I?

sentences.english: But, if scientists are telling us taste is largely genetic, it might seem pointless for wine experts or food critics to tell us what tastes good.
sentences.korean: 하지만 과학자들이 미각이 대체로 유전적이라고 한다면요.

sentences.english: Pointless means something that has no use, purpose or meaning.
Well, TV food critic David Myers wouldn't agree.
sentences.korean: 음, TV 음식 비평가 데이비드 마이어스는 동의하지 않을 거예요.

sentences.english: Here he speaks about the value of what he does:
I think there is clearly a case for that, but taste is subjective.
sentences.korean: 분명히 그런 면이 있지만, 미각은 주관적입니다.

quiz.question: What does '무의미한, 쓸데없는' mean in English?

sentences.english: Food speaks to us in different ways.
My job is never to tell you what to eat, that would be pointless.
sentences.korean: 내 일은 뭘 먹으라고 말하는 게 절대 아니에요, 그건 무의미할 거예요.

sentences.english: I would say that, wouldn't I?
This phrase means something like, 'of course I would say that'.
sentences.korean: 이 표현은 '물론 저는 그렇게 말하겠죠' 같은 뜻입니다.

sentences.english: It implies there's some reason that what the speaker is saying is obviously biased towards them.
sentences.korean: 이 표현은 '물론 저는 그렇게 말하겠죠' 같은 뜻이에요

quiz.question: What does '내가 그렇게 말할 수밖에 (편향 인정)' mean in English?
options: I would say that, wouldn't I?

sentences.english: David admits that taste is subjective, based on personal opinions and feelings rather than on facts.
sentences.korean: 데이빗은 미각이 개인 의견과 감정에 기반한 주관적인 것이라고 인정합니다.

sentences.english: I think it's time to reveal the answer to your question, Phil.
Right. I asked for Britain's most popular flavour of crisp, and you said, cheese and onion which was… the correct answer!
sentences.korean: 네, 영국에서 가장 인기 있는 과자 맛을 물었었고, 치즈 앤 어니언이 정답이었습니다!

sentences.english: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned in this programme starting with the phrase to broaden or expand your horizons meaning 'to explore something in a new way which increases your knowledge and understanding of it'.
To chastise means to strongly punish or criticise.

quiz.question: What does '시야를 넓히다' mean in English?

sentences.english: A colour-blind person is unable to distinguish certain colours especially greens and reds.
sentences.korean: 색맹인 사람은 특정 색상을 구분하지 못합니다.

sentences.english: Something which is pointless, has no purpose or meaning and is a waste of time.
sentences.korean: 'pointless'한 것은 목적이나 의미가 없고 시간 낭비입니다.

sentences.english: The phrase 'I would say that, wouldn't I?' is a tag question meaning the speaker might be biased about what they're saying.
sentences.korean: 'I would say that, wouldn't I?'는 화자가 편향될 수 있다는 의미의 부가 의문문입니다.

sentences.english: And finally, subjective means based on personal opinions and feelings rather than facts.
sentences.korean: 마지막으로, 'subjective'는 사실이 아닌 개인적 의견과 감정에 기반한다는 뜻입니다.

sentences.english: Once again, our six minutes are up.
Bye for now!
sentences.korean: 사실이 아닌 개인적 의견과 감정에 기반한다는 뜻이에요

sentences.english: Bye!

quiz.question: What does '꾸짖다, 비난하다' mean in English?
